title: Equation
short: A statement that two expressions are equal, like x² − 4 = 0. Solving it finds the values that make it true.
full:
An equation asserts that two expressions have the same value, joined by an equals sign, such as 2x + 1 = 7. Solving an equation means finding every value of the variable that makes the statement true — its solutions or roots.

A standard tactic is to move everything to one side so the equation reads 'expression = 0', then factor or apply a formula. In the CAS tab's Solve operation you can write the two sides directly (left = right).

Equations are pure algebra and carry no units.
